violations.text: The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in need of repair/resurfacing... - The floor tiles in the upstairs dry storage area were observed to be in poor repair (Tiles are observed to be chipped/missing exposing the wood subfloor). - Wood trim surfaces were observed need of resealing. Paint observed to be worn. (PIC made aware of approved coatings for interior surfaces.) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is licensed/registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Per the PIC, the applications for Certified Food Safety Manger Licenses are ready for submission. Applications are to be submitted as soon as possible. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024

The floor tiles in the upstairs dry storage area were observed to be in poor repair (Tiles are observed to be chipped/missing exposing the wood subfloor). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2024

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Bucket was emptied and refilled with proper sanitizer solution. Per PIC, part has been ordered for dispenser to dispense at appropriate ppm. Establishment to test sanitizer solution after using dispenser to ensure it is adequate ppm. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2023

The door to the outside, located in the dry storage area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. PIC stated door would be sealed with molding. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2023

Various foods were observed held above 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All foods prepared before today were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Solution must be diluted to proper ppm. Inspector recommends adjusting the dispenser to dispense adequate ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The door to the outside, located in the dry storage area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2023
The reach-in unit was not in holding an adequate ambient temperature. Unit to be serviced/repaired/adjusted prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The sink (3-bay sink) used for food preparation is lacking an air gap (currently has air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
